def: s.m. Essential, substantial; vigorous, strong, hard excellent; good, sound; best; real, true, genuine; thoroughly proved, irrefragable

n. m. 1. Juice. 2. Sap; strength; vigour. 3. Pith ( tat 1, 2.). 4. Value; worth; importance; weight; excellence. 1. Haoṅ sajnī jānat nahīṅ pīyā bichhran kī sār, Jiā bichhran se hai kaṭhan, pīyā bichhran kī bār . My dear! to part from one's beloved I knew not was such pain, To part from life is parting from one's love as hard again. 2. Vihaṅs prahlād ko god biṭhār-ke, kahī, mukh chumī 'sut, paṛho kahā? ' 'Rām hī nām sab paṛhāṅ meṅ sār hai, paṛheṅ ham.' Soī suni hirde dāhā . Vishrām Sāgar. And Prahlad smiling took and kissed the boy and said 'what do you read?' 'Ram knowledge's essence is, but Ram I read.' The words his heart did bleed. 5. A cowshed. Gau-sār . 6. Manure ( khāt ). 7. A chess board. 8. A piece at chausar , chess, backgammon, etc. sār jānnā , v. n. To appreciate; esteem.
s. f. 1. ( सार ) Manure. 2. Iron. 3. Pith, essence, quintessence. 4. Value, worth. 5. ( शार ) The piece used in playing chaupaṛ. 6. See S̱. سر sar.

Death hovers over our heads. 3. Sir sc utre bāl, gū meṅ jāo, yā mūt meṅ jāo! Prov. The hair taken from your head in excrement may go, in urinal may go! 4. Ek būṛhī, Shaitān kī khālā, Sar safaid, aur mūṅh hai kālā . Riddle. Ans. Ākh kī buṛhiyā . A woman old, she is the Devil's aunt, Her head is white, her face is black, you 'll gránt. 5. Sir pe patthar, peṭ meṅ unglī . Riddle. Ans. Aṅgūṭhī a ring. A stone on the head, the finger in its stomach. 6. Sir naqd naukrī udhār . Prov. Your head in cash you tender, service on credit render. 7. Ek sir, hazār saudā . Prov. One head, a thousand things upon it laid! 2. The top; crown; pinnacle. 3. Responsibility; liability. n. m. S. श्राद्ध The funeral rites of Hindus performed on certain days in honor of one's ancestors; obsequies of the dead. Āj kyā bāp kā sarādh kīyā thā? (Said of a feast). ekādash sarāddh . Presentation of offerings to the deceased on the 11th day after his decease, when Brahmans are fed, and the period of uncleanness for a Brahman terminates. pret sarāddh, dash pinḍ sarāddh , n. m. An offering of a pind or ball of rice to a person deceased by the next of kin, increasing the number daily for ten successive days until they amount to ten. sapinḍī sarāddh , n. m. barsī , n. f. The presentation of the pinḍ to the deceased and his progenitors in both lines on the first anniversary after one's death. māsik sarāddh , n. m. hāṅḍī , n. f. The monthly srādh performed for one year.

ety: Prk. सलाहणअं; S. श्लाघनीयं, rt. श्लाघ्
def: v.t. To praise, magnify, commend, applaud, celebrate, eulogize; to approve, appreciate, admire; — s.m. = sarāhan.
more: v. a. S. श्लाघा Pr. salāhā from शलघ to praise. 1. To praise; commend; applaud. Kadhī sarāhā man ne nāhīṅ, āchhā bhī khā-kar tan ne; Dukh is ghar māṅ sadā batāyā, sukhṛā bhī pā-kar tan ne! W. Rus. Wom. song. You never gave me any praise when you have had good fare, Of ills complaining e'er though you have had of joy your share. 2. To appreciate; admire. Kānī ko kaun sarāhve? Kānī kā bāvā . Prov. Whoe'er the one-eyed girl doth praise? Her father. 3. To flatter; butter; beslaver. Phūhaṛ kā mā/?/ sarāh sarāh khāīye . Prov. Flatter the fool and you may have her money.
v. a. To praise, commend, approve, applaud, celebrate.
wety: Inherited from Middle Hindi [Term?] (c. 1611), from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀲𑀮𑀸𑀳𑀤𑀺 (salāhadi), from Sanskrit श्लाघते (ślā́ghate).

ety: sarak˚ = Prk. सरक्क(इ), or सरक्के(इ), fr. S. सर+कृ; — cf. saṛaknā
def: v.n. To be moved, to be displaced; to move, shift, slip, get out of place; to stir, budge, get out of the way, remove; to flinch, draw back, recede, retire, give way, give place
more: v. n. 1. See sarkānā , (1, 2). 1. Nek eṅ saraki āi, yār! mohī kā jāṛo lagat hai! E. Wom. Ṭhumrī . A little nearer this side, love! I am so cold. 2. Andar chilvan, bāhar chilvan, bīch kalejā dhaṛke, Amīr Khusro yoṅ kaheṅ, woh do do uṅgal sarke . Riddle. Ans. Qaiṅchī . Scissors. Inside and out a curtain see, betwixt a fluttering heart, Its steps are jerking starts just fingers two apart. 2. To move off; turn aside; give way. Sarak, balamvā! morī biṅdyā hirānī, Sagri sejaṛyāṅ ḍhūṅḍat hūṅ, milat nāhīṅ . Song. Ṭhumrī . Just move a little husband, pray, my spangle's dropped somewhere, I've looked all o'er the bed, I cannot find it anywhere. 3. To draw back; recede; retire. 4. To slide; glide. Khule-bandoṅ huī āmad sahar kī, Uṭhā dāman ko shab āge se sarkī . Fidvī. The dawn unveiled appeared to view, Her skirt tucked in the night withdrew.
v. a. 1. To sniffle. 2. To gulp down. 3. To draw a sword from the scabbard.
v. n. To be moved, to remove, move, stir, to get out of the way, budge.
subs: v.n. (intens.), To move away, to get out of place, to slip, &c. ( = saraknā).

adj. & s.m. Of a variegated colour, variegated, spotted, gaudy; — the spotted deer; a deer; the ćātak, Cuculus melanoleucus; the Koʼil or Indian cuckoo; the peacock; the cry of the peacock; a kind of crane, Ardea sarunga; a large bee; a snake; a cloud; water; name of a rāg or musical mode (the third of the Rāgas; — it is sung at midday); a lion; an elephant; a woman; a tree; a lamp; an umbrella, parasol; a garment, cloth; hair; a lotus; a flower; a conch-shell; a sort of musical instrument; an ornament, a jewel; gold; a bow; sandal; camphor; manure; an epithet of Śiva; an epithet of Kām-deva; name of a country

Sāraṅg ne sāraṅg gaho, sāraṅg bolo āë, Jo sāraṅg sāraṅg kahe, sāraṅg muṅh te jāë . Dohā. A peacock caught a snake, the clouds their thunder rolled, If but the peacock scream the snake drops from his hold. (The peacock is said to scream and dance with joy at the sound of thunder).

s. m. 1. Name of a musical mode or rāg. 2. A peacock. 3. A snake, a serpent. 4. A cloud. 5. The cry of a peacock. 6. A deer. 7. A woman. 8. Water. 9. The name of a country. 10. A lamp. 11. The Nymphæa lotus. 12. (for शार्ङ ) The bow of Vish̤ṇu. سارنگ ني سارنگ گـہيوسارنگ بوليوآي جوسارنگ سارنگ کـہي سارنگ منـہہ تين جاي Sārang ne sārang gahyo, sārang bolyo āʼe; jau sārang sārang kahe, sārang muṅh teṅ jāʼe. A peacock seized a snake; a cloud thundered: if the peacock utter his cry, the snake will escape from his mouth. (It is said to be a property of the peacock that on hearing thunder he utters a cry of pleasure and dances for joy).

s.f. A chess-man, a piece at chess or backgammon; — the Maina, &c. ( = sārikā, q.v.).
adj. Going, proceeding, resorting to; — having the essence or substance of (used as last member of compounds).
s.f. Cream.
s.f. A kind of dress worn by Hindū women, a long piece of cloth wrapped round the body and passed over the head (in some parts of India it is worn in the manner of a lungī, q.v.).
more: s. f. See S̱. ساري sign. 2. ¶ sāṛe, adj. See ساڙهي sāṛhe.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀲𑀸𑀟𑀻 (sāḍī), from Sanskrit शाटी (śāṭī), from शाट (śāṭa, “strip of cloth”). Doublet of شَال (śāl) through Persian. Compare Punjabi ਸਾੜ੍ਹੀ (sāṛhī) / ساڑھی (sāṛhī), Sindhi ساڙهيِ (sāṛhyi) / साढ़ी, Gujarati સાડી (sāḍī), Marathi साडी (sāḍī), Bengali শাড়ি (śaṛi), Assamese শাৰী (xari), Sylheti ꠢꠣꠠꠤ (haṛi), Malayalam സാരി (sāri).

def: s.f. A reed (of which arrows are made); shaft (of an arrow); — wire (of gold, &c. ( = sariyā, q.v.).

ety: sar, q.v.+Zend aff. i = S. इ
def: s.f. Chiefship; — adj. Good.
adj. Quick, expeditious, swift; nimble; ready
more: adj. سرع was quick. Quick; ready.
(from سرع) adj. Quick, nimble, ready. سريع التائير sarīʼuﻌ-t-tās̤īr, Ready, penetrating, active (a medicine). سريع الزوال sarīʼuﻌ-z-zawāl, Soon passing away, fading, frail.
subs: adj. Quick in answering, ready with an answer
adj. Quick in taking effect, penetrating, active (a medicine)
adj. Quick or brisk in movement or action, quick, swift, fleet, rapid
adj. Quick or ready in reckoning (God)
adj. Soon passing away, quickly perishing, transient, fading, frail
adj. Quickly digested, easy of digestion.
wety: Borrowed from Arabic سَرِيع (sarīʕ).

def: s.m. A piece or man (at chess, or backgammon, &c.); — an elephant's housings; — fraud; — s.f. The mainā ( = śārī, q.v.).
s.f. Kuśa grass; — the Mainā, or Gracula religiosa; an arrow.
s.f. corr. of ساڙي sāṛī, q.v.
more: s. f. 1. (from सार ) Cream. 2. ( शाटी ) A dress consisting of one piece of cloth worn by Hindū women round the body and passing over the head.
(part. act. of سري) adj. 1. Passing, penetrating, pervading, flowing. 2. Infecting, contagious, infectious.

def: s.f. Cascade, waterfall; water-course, channel for water (in a garden, &c.), a river; — sari-tīr, s.m. Bank or margin of a river.
more: s. f. 1. The shaft of an arrow. 2. A reed of which arrows are made.

ety: for A. سارٍ sāriṉ, act. part. of سري 'to travel by night,' &c.
def: part. adj. Passing; flowing; circulating; extending (to); penetrating, pervading; infecting, contagious.
s.m. corr. of sharīfa, q.v. s.m. The custard-apple, Annona squamosa.
s.m. The gamut; the descending scale; sol-fa-ing, solmization.
more: n. f. S. सरिगम. 1. Sol-fa-ing; solmization. 2. The gamut; the descending scale.

def: s. m. The ear.

ety: v.n. fr. صرح 'to be pure, or clear,' &c.
def: adj. & adv. Clear, apparent, evident, manifest, plain, palpable; gross; — clearly, plainly, evidently, &c.; out and out, absolutely, altogether, in toto (e.g. ṣarīḥ mukarnā, 'to deny absolutely or in toto')
more: adj. صرح was clear 1. See صاف 8, 10, 15. Gross; great; palpable. sarīh mukarnā , v. n. To deny absolutely. zurūrat-i-sarīh . Manifest necessity.
(from صرح) adj. Apparent, evident, clear, palpable.

def: adv. Clearly, &c. ( = ṣarīḥ, q.v.). adj. & adv. Clear, apparent, evident, manifest, plain, palpable; gross; — clearly, plainly, evidently, &c.; out and out, absolutely, altogether, in toto (e.g. ṣarīḥ mukarnā, 'to deny absolutely or in toto')
more: adv. 1. Expressly; palpably ( sāf 1, 2). 2. Manifestly; evidently. az rūe alfāz-i-sarīhī . In express terms.

def: adj. & adv. = ṣarīḥ, q.v. adj. & adv. Clear, apparent, evident, manifest, plain, palpable; gross; — clearly, plainly, evidently, &c.; out and out, absolutely, altogether, in toto (e.g. ṣarīḥ mukarnā, 'to deny absolutely or in toto')

s. f. Name of a bird (Turdus salica: Buch. M. S. ): also, a mainā (Gracula religiosa).

def: s.f. = sārikā, q.v. s.f. A starling, Turdus salica; — the Mainā, Gracula religiosa; — the Śāmā, q.v.
adj. &c. = next, q.v.
more: adj. S. सदृक्ष, Pr. sarichchham . 1. A particle of similitude. 2. Like; resembling; so; as. Tum sarīke bahutere dekhe haiṅ .

def: adj. & postpn. = sarīka, or sarīkhā, q.v. s.f. A starling, Turdus salica; — the Mainā, Gracula religiosa; — the Śāmā, q.v.

def: s.m. Stealing; theft, robbery larceny; plagiarism (syn. ćorī).
more: (from سرق To steal) s. m. Plagiarism, theft.

r: sārikh, sārikā, sārkā, sārikhā, sārkhā, sarīkhā
def: = H سارکا सारिका sārikā, सारका sārkā, = H سارکها सारिखा sārikhā, सारखा sārkhā, aff. or postpn. = sarīkhā, q.v.

ety: Prk. सरिच्छो; S. सदृक्षः; cf. also S. सादृक्ष्यं; Prk. सारिक्खं
def: s.m. Similitude; pattern; — adj. (f. -ī), & postpn. (governing the formative, or the genitive), Like, resembling, after the pattern or type (of), such as, as, so (e.g. ham-sarīkhī abalā, 'such weak women as we are'; tum-sarīkhe mūrkhoṅ-ko pakaṛne-ke liye, 'for the purpose of catching fools like you'; us-sarīkhe-ko mat māno, 'don't heed the like of him,' 'don't heed a fellow like that').

r: sārikhā, sārkhā, sārikā, sārkā, sārikh, sarīkhā
def: = H سارکا सारिका sārikā, सारका sārkā, = H سارکهہ सारिख sārikh, aff. or postpn. = sarīkhā, q.v.
v.t. To liken; — v.n. To be like, to resemble.

ety: Prk. सडिअइल्लो and सडिअइल्लओ; S. शदित+इलः and इल +कः
def: adj. = saṛā, q.v. adj. Opulent, rich; — s.m. The earth.
s.m. (dialec.) A violin, &c. ( = sāraṅgī, q.v.). s.f. A musical instrument like a fiddle.
more: (perhaps, from सारंगी ) s. (dakh.) A violin, fiddle ( Harris ).

def: s.m. A couch-frame, bedstead, cot, a raised couch; a throne.
more: n. m. S. शरीर See بدن 1. Sarīroṅ sukh raheṅ! Benediction. 2. Najaryā se tor jīarā bhar doṅ, chhūe na dūṅgī sarīr . E. Wom. song. I with my eyes your soul will fill, but let you touch me I ne'er will. sarīr-bandhak , n. m. A hostage ( ol ). sarīr dhāran k. , v. n. To be embodied, incarnate. sarīr-ḍanḍ , n. m. Corporal punishment. sarīr-sambandh . Relation by blood. sarīr kī choṭ , n. f. Bodily hurt; personal violence.
s. m. Body of any animated being.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian سریر (sarīr), from Arabic سَرِير (sarīr).
